title: aita for telling my uber driver i didn't want to talk to him?
text: i was taking a uber to work and this dude wouldn't stop talking. he was too friendly. and he has a really thick accent so i can't even understand him that well. at a certain point i just start using my phone and start not really paying attention to what he is saying. i just say "yeah""wow""really?" when he says stuff. guy gets annoyed at this and starts asking me if something is wrong. i say no and he says i seem to be uninterested in our conversation. i tell him the truth and that i'm just not interested in talking right now. he gives a little angry huff and stops talking. i get to my stop and get out. i tell him thanks. he says i got to work on my attitude problems and drives away. i'm left there dumbfounded and wondering if i was the asshole here. was i?

storyboard.content: I was taking an Uber to work. The driver wouldn't stop talking and was very friendly. He also had a thick accent, making it hard for me to understand him.
I started using my phone and stopped paying real attention to what he was saying, just giving minimal responses like 'yeah,' 'wow,' and 'really?'
He got annoyed and started asking me if something was wrong, saying I seemed uninterested in our conversation.
I told him the truth — that I'm just not interested in talking right now.
He gave an angry huff and stopped talking. When I got out and thanked him, he told me I need to work on my attitude problems and drove away.
I was just trying to get to work and wasn't looking for a conversation.
